title: Discrete-time survival expert over each stint
summary: `PitNextLap` is a one-step hazard from an underlying time-to-pit process.
rationale: `PitNextLap` is a one-step hazard from an underlying time-to-pit process. Discrete-time survival literature shows person-period reformulation lets standard classifiers estimate conditional event hazards while handling censoring and the run-up to an event more faithfully than a flat binary label.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race-stint, expand rows up to pit or censor, mark the terminal interval as the event, include baseline time terms for tyre age/lap-in-stint/race progress, and fit a discrete hazard model (logit or complementary log-log). Convert the predicted hazard at the current row back to next-lap pit probability and stack it with tree models.
expected_effect: Can capture the full stop-horizon structure and duration dependence, yielding a meaningfully different expert from current pointwise GBDTs.
risk: Row expansion increases training size a lot; careless censoring or horizon construction can create leakage or class imbalance problems.
